rawAwsEvidence.cloudTrailPersistencePolicy: Persist only allowlisted event IDs, names and timestamps. Do not persist raw CloudTrailEvent response payloads because STS events can contain temporary workload credential material.

diagnosis: The targeted-only EC2 user data called blocking `systemctl restart ecs` from cloud-init modules:final. That command never returned before CloudFormation rollback terminated the instance about 224 seconds after launch; the console then recorded `Job for ecs.service canceled` and scripts-user failure. Because the ECS agent never registered, the ClickHouse ECS service could not place a task and the one permitted runtime deploy rolled back.

scope.reason: The strict Phase 7 stack does not call `systemctl restart ecs` from cloud-init and already gates ECS services on ASG and exact capacity-provider registration readiness signals.

smallestFix: Remove the blocking ECS restart from the targeted user data. Reuse the strict stack's two-stage readiness sequence: signal ASG host readiness only after the local ECS metadata endpoint is live, then signal a wait condition only after DescribeContainerInstances reports agentConnected=true, status=ACTIVE and the exact run-owned capacityProviderName. Make the targeted ClickHouse ECS service depend on that wait condition.
requiredRegression: targeted user data does not contain `systemctl restart ecs`
targeted ASG has a 10-minute ResourceSignal CreationPolicy
targeted stack has one WaitCondition and one WaitConditionHandle
targeted service DependsOn the exact ASG, capacity-provider association and registration WaitCondition
targeted user data polls local ECS metadata and exact DescribeContainerInstances registration
decoded LaunchTemplate UserData remains at or below 15360 bytes
